examples.name: Two-state switch
examples.description: The textbook intro. Two states with symmetric switching probabilities.

examples.name: Weather (3-state)
examples.description: Sunny, cloudy, rainy. Classic textbook example of a 3-state Markov chain.

examples.name: Snakes & Ladders
examples.description: The classic board game as a Markov chain. States 0-100. Snakes and ladders are teleportations; each turn you roll a fair die (1-6). State 0 is start, 100 is absorbing.

examples.name: Random walk on {-5..5}
examples.description: Simple random walk on integers truncated to {-5,-4,...,4,5}. Reflecting boundaries: at ±5 you stay with prob 0.5.

examples.name: Birth-death chain
examples.description: Population chain on {0,1,...,6}. State 0 is absorbing (extinction). Each step: birth with prob 0.6, death with prob 0.4 (except boundaries).

examples.name: Cat and mouse
examples.description: 4-state predator-prey toy. States: both alive, cat only, mouse only, neither. Transitions model capture and escape probabilities.

examples.name: Blank (3 states)
examples.description: Start from scratch. Edit the transition matrix directly; rows re-normalize automatically.
